market_overview.headline: Market rallied on Iran diplomacy hopes as both SPY and QQQ broke above key EMAs with solid momentum.
market_overview.detail: 42% of stocks in Full Bull mode with 67% of names showing bullish EMA structures. Energy leads with 100% bullish participation while tech weakness shows signs of stabilizing.

reversal_candidates.headline: Bear Rally + Bullish Crossover Reversal Setups

items.note: Mega-cap tech showing early reversal signals as 8W EMA approaches key bullish crossover.

momentum_themes.headline: Tech Hardware & Optical Components Leading Acceleration
momentum_themes.detail: Electronic technology dominance with optical/storage plays like SNDK, LITE, and CIEN showing explosive momentum. Energy minerals maintaining 100% bullish participation as Iran tensions support commodity complex.

sector_analysis.headline: Energy Dominance While Tech Shows Mixed Signals

strongest.note: Perfect bullish participation driven by Iran oil embargo and geopolitical tensions.
Industrial strength with 94% bullish names showing economic resilience.
Tech hardware outperforming software with 89% bullish participation.

weakest.note: Software sector under heavy pressure with 81% bearish participation.
Banks showing mixed signals despite early reversal setups in major names.
Healthcare weakness continues with two-thirds of names in bearish structures.

sector_analysis.detail: Clear rotation from software/services into energy and hardware as Iran tensions support commodity complex. Financials showing early reversal signals despite sector-wide weakness.

items.text: 81% of tech services names remain in bearish EMA structures despite market rally
BTC still in bear rally mode with -43% deviation from fair value suggests crypto headwinds persist
Only 8 bull breakdowns but watch for acceleration if Iran diplomacy succeeds and energy reverses
High relative volume in several pullbacks could signal institutional distribution

news_drivers.headline: What Moved Markets
news_drivers.summary: Markets rallied on renewed hopes for US-Iran diplomatic talks as Trump suggested negotiations could resume within days. Iran oil embargo and Strait of Hormuz tensions drove energy sector strength while geopolitical uncertainty supported broader risk-on sentiment.

items.text: Iran diplomacy optimism drove broad market rally as negotiation hopes reduced geopolitical risk premium
US non-renewal of Iranian oil waivers and Hormuz blockade boosted energy stocks across the board
Oil price decline on diplomatic hopes created sector rotation from energy into growth names
IMF growth downgrades for Middle East highlighted economic impact of regional conflict
United-American merger speculation added transportation sector interest amid broader rally
